meta.comment:
Multi-provider deployed model set for the cluster scorer. Each entry maps a deployable (model, provider) target to the OpenRouterBench column whose scores trained its ranking row in rankings.json. OpenRouterBench was captured 2025-Q4, before GPT-5.5 (April 2026) and Gemini 3 series shipped, so all frontier OpenAI/Google entries are proxy: true and inherit ranking signal from the closest bench column they generationally derive from. Replace with direct bench columns when an updated bench arrives. Anthropic stays on the Sept-2025 Claude 4 line; the user's ask was to refresh OpenAI/Google to the latest frontier.

Runtime argmax operates over this list filtered to providers whose API key is registered at boot — see cluster.NewScorer's availableProviders argument. Re-run router/scripts/train_cluster_router.py whenever this file changes.

deployed_models.proxy_note: no claude-haiku column in OpenRouterBench; gemini-2.5-flash stands in as the cheapest small-tier proxy until D3 traffic accumulates
no claude-opus column in OpenRouterBench; gpt-5 is the closest frontier proxy

GPT-5.5 (April 2026) ships after the bench cutoff; use gpt-5's column as the closest generational proxy. gpt-5.5 lists at 2x the price of gpt-5, so the α-blend will favor gpt-5 when bench scores tie — that's intentional given we have no direct evidence gpt-5.5 outperforms gpt-5 on these prompts

Gemini 3.1 Pro Preview supersedes Gemini 3 Pro Preview (deprecated 2026-03-09). Bench cutoff predates the 3.x family so we proxy from gemini-2.5-pro's column. Drop the -preview suffix once Google ships GA

Gemini 3 Flash Preview shipped post-bench; use gemini-2.5-flash's column. The bench scores are conservative — Gemini 3 Flash is reportedly stronger and cheaper, so the α-blend will likely under-pick it until D3 traffic recalibrates
Cheapest Google entry; same column as gemini-3-flash-preview but lower cost rebalances the α-blend toward this tier on cost-sensitive clusters
